blocks.content: By Wednesday, Kit had figured out that seventh grade ran on a different set of rules than sixth grade and nobody had told him what they were.
He and Daisy shared homeroom and English, but science and math split them — those were the two longest periods of the day.
He was at his locker after third period when someone stopped next to him at the empty locker two down.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: Hey. You're Kit, right? Kit Jenkins?

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: I'm Brooke. Brooke Hadley. I moved here in June — I think you live like two streets from my cousin?

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: He said you're the one who climbed the water tower.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: He said a lot of things.
She smiled at him — easy, direct — and went to class. Kit stood there a half second, then went to class.
